figures.caption: Fig. 4. Distribution of word counts in questions, ground-truth answers and predictions of medical 3D VLMs.

Fig. 1. Current 3D medical VLMs struggle to answer spatially grounded questions, highlighting limitations in spatial reasoning and raising concerns about their reliability for clinically meaningful use.
Table 1. Performance of Medical 3D VLMs on the CT-SpatialVQA Benchmark. - Mention Accuracy in the table

Fig. 5. (Left) Radar plot showing model performance across six spatial reasoning categories in CT-SpatialVQA (LLM-as-Jury). (Right). Spatial categories considered for our evaluation. Each category captures a clinically relevant spatial primitive required for preserving volumetric spatial integrity in medical VLMs.

Fig. 2. An Overview of CT-SpatialVQA (top) The dataset generation pipeline utilizes 3D CT scan and paired radiology report to prompt a "QA Generator LLM", synthesizing candidate spatially-grounded QA pairs. An independent QA Validator LLM filters the candidates, and the remaining pairs are then verified by medical human auditors to produce the final benchmark dataset. (bottom) The 3D medical VLM evaluation protocol tests a given model by inputting a 3D CT scan and a spatiallygrounded question. The model’s generated answer is compared to the ground truth using a dual-track assessment system, where LLM-based evaluation and traditional text metrics are conducted independently to produce the final performance score.
